name: Union Yes
description_short: Union Yes Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Orange, California, established in 1981, serving over 21,700 members with $86.3M in assets.
description_long:
Union Yes Federal Credit Union was established in 1981 and is headquartered in Orange, California. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution dedicated to serving its membership base. The credit union has grown to steward over $86.3 million in assets and maintain a membership of over 21,700 loyal members, reflecting its role as a community-focused financial institution.

Union Yes offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and mortgage options. Members have access to online banking, mobile banking apps for iOS and Android, bill pay, e-statements, wire transfers, and online loan payments. The credit union also provides financial education resources and financial counseling services to support member financial wellness.

What distinguishes Union Yes is its intimate community focus with a single branch location and approximately 12 dedicated employees, creating an environment where members can develop personal relationships with staff. Members consistently report friendly and knowledgeable service, with reviewers noting the small-town credit union atmosphere where employees are known on a first-name basis. The credit union maintains strong member satisfaction, with a 5.0 rating based on available reviews praising customer service quality and approachability.

However, prospective members should be aware that Union Yes operates as a small, single-branch institution with limited physical presence and ATM access (1 ATM), which may restrict convenience for members requiring multiple locations or extensive ATM networks. Additionally, some members have reported slow website performance when accessing online banking. Membership eligibility is determined by common bond requirements specific to Union Yes, meaning not all consumers may be eligible to join. The institution's smaller asset base and limited branch network mean it may offer fewer specialized services or competitive rates compared to larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established 45-year history since 1981 with $86.3M in assets and 21,700+ members
Personal relationship-focused service with staff known on first-name basis, rated 5.0 stars across reviews
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and mortgages
Mobile banking apps available for both iOS and Android with online banking, bill pay, and e-statements
Financial counseling and financial education services included for members
Small, community-oriented credit union model emphasizing member service over profit maximization
Wire transfers, money market accounts, and IRA/KEOGH accounts available
cons: Single branch location in Orange, California with only 1 ATM, severely limiting physical access and convenience
Website and online banking portal reported as slow by members, creating frustration with digital access
Membership eligibility based on specific common bonds, making it unavailable to all consumers
Limited scale compared to regional or national credit unions or banks, potentially restricting specialized services or competitive rates
Lobby currently closed at time of data collection, further limiting member access options
best_for: Consumers in the Orange, California area who are eligible for membership and value personal relationships with financial staff
Members seeking a full-service credit union with mortgages, business loans, and credit cards in a community-focused environment
Individuals who prioritize friendly, knowledgeable service and small-town banking atmosphere over extensive branch networks
Eligible members looking for comprehensive financial education and counseling services alongside standard banking products
diagnosis: Union Yes Federal Credit Union is best suited for eligible consumers in Orange, California who value personalized service, community relationships, and a comprehensive product suite over branch convenience and extensive ATM networks. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted by common bond requirements, and the single-location model with reported website speed issues may frustrate members seeking high digital convenience or geographic flexibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
